states.name: Idle
states.description: Initial state with no active commissioning approval request

states.name: CASE_Session_Validation_Failed
states.description: RequestCommissioningApproval received without CASE session

states.name: Device_Category_Check_Failed
states.description: Client attempted to request approval for unsupported device category

states.name: Duplicate_RequestID_Detected
states.description: Client reused RequestID while previous request still pending

states.name: Approval_Request_Accepted
states.description: Server accepted RequestCommissioningApproval and will generate event

state_variables.request_record: struct{RequestID, ClientNodeID, VendorID, ProductID, Label, FabricID}

states.name: Awaiting_User_Approval
states.description: Server optionally requesting user approval for commissioning

states.name: Approval_Granted
states.description: CommissioningRequestResult event generated with StatusCode SUCCESS

states.name: Approval_Denied
states.description: CommissioningRequestResult event generated with StatusCode FAILURE

states.name: Approval_Timeout
states.description: User approval timed out, CommissioningRequestResult with StatusCode TIMEOUT

states.name: CommissionNode_CASE_Validation_Failed
states.description: CommissionNode received without CASE session

states.name: CommissionNode_Identity_Mismatch
states.description: CommissionNode from different NodeID or Fabric than approval

states.name: CommissionNode_RequestID_Invalid
states.description: CommissionNode RequestID not found in approved requests

states.name: CommissionNode_No_Approval_Success
states.description: CommissionNode received but no SUCCESS event for RequestID

states.name: CommissionNode_Already_Used
states.description: CommissionNode for RequestID already responded with ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow

states.name: Commissioning_Window_Preparation
states.description: Server preparing ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow response

states.name: PAKE_Parameters_Invalid
states.description: ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow parameters out of valid range
invariants: Iterations NOT IN [1000, 100000] OR length(Salt) NOT IN [16, 32] OR Discriminator > 4095

states.name: Server_Window_Response_Sent
states.description: Server sent ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow to client

states.name: Client_Awaiting_Response
states.description: Client waiting for ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow within timeout

states.name: Client_Response_Timeout
states.description: Client timeout waiting for ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow

states.name: Client_Window_Opening
states.description: Client received ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow and opening window

states.name: Client_Device_Mismatch
states.description: Client cannot find device matching VendorID and ProductID
invariants: device_found == false OR device.VendorID != approval.VendorID OR device.ProductID != approval.ProductID

states.name: Client_Commissioning_Window_Open
states.description: Client opened commissioning window on target device

states.name: Server_Commissioning_Device
states.description: Server commissioning device through opened window

states.name: Device_Attestation_Verification
states.description: Server verifying Device Attestation Certificate and Basic Information

states.name: Device_Identity_Mismatch
states.description: Device Basic Information VendorID/ProductID does not match approval

states.name: Commissioning_Aborted_With_Error
states.description: Server aborted commissioning and should indicate error to user

states.name: Commissioning_Complete
states.description: Device successfully commissioned with verified identity

transitions.guard_condition: session_type == CASE && device_category NOT IN SupportedDeviceCategories
session_type == CASE && device_category IN SupportedDeviceCategories && check_duplicate_request(RequestID, ClientNodeID, pending_requests) == true
session_type == CASE && device_category IN SupportedDeviceCategories && check_duplicate_request(RequestID, ClientNodeID, pending_requests) == false
actions: store_approval_request(RequestID, ClientNodeID, VendorID, ProductID, Label, FabricID)

transitions.guard_condition: session_type == CASE && CommissionNode.NodeID == Approval.NodeID && CommissionNode.FabricID == Approval.FabricID && validate_commission_node_requestid(CommissionNode.RequestID, approved_requests) == false

session_type == CASE && CommissionNode.NodeID == Approval.NodeID && CommissionNode.FabricID == Approval.FabricID && validate_commission_node_requestid(CommissionNode.RequestID, approved_requests) == true && check_approval_success(CommissionNode.RequestID, event_history) == false

session_type == CASE && CommissionNode.NodeID == Approval.NodeID && CommissionNode.FabricID == Approval.FabricID && validate_commission_node_requestid(CommissionNode.RequestID, approved_requests) == true && check_approval_success(CommissionNode.RequestID, event_history) == true && check_already_responded(CommissionNode.RequestID, response_history) == true && new_approval_received == false

session_type == CASE && CommissionNode.NodeID == Approval.NodeID && CommissionNode.FabricID == Approval.FabricID && validate_commission_node_requestid(CommissionNode.RequestID, approved_requests) == true && check_approval_success(CommissionNode.RequestID, event_history) == true && (check_already_responded(CommissionNode.RequestID, response_history) == false || new_approval_received == true)

actions: open_commissioning_window(target_device, window_params.PAKEPasscodeVerifier, window_params.Discriminator, window_params.Iterations, window_params.Salt, window_params.CommissioningTimeout)

functions.name: check_case_session

parameters.name: session_context
parameters.description: Current session context containing session type and authentication status

functions.description: Verifies that the current session is a CASE (Certificate Authenticated Session Establishment) session with proper authentication credentials
functions.algorithm_detail: Check session_context.type field. Return true if session_context.type == CASE and session is authenticated, false otherwise
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in all transitions receiving RequestCommissioningApproval or CommissionNode to enforce CASE session requirement

functions.name: check_device_category_support

parameters.name: device_category
parameters.description: Device category bit to check (e.g., FabricSynchronization bit 0)

functions.description: Checks if the requested device category is supported by this Commissioner Control Cluster server
functions.algorithm_detail: Perform bitwise AND: (SupportedDeviceCategories & (1 << device_category)). Return true if result is non-zero, false otherwise
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Idle->Device_Category_Check_Failed transition to validate client's request against server capabilities

functions.name: check_duplicate_request

parameters.name: pending_requests
parameters.description: Server's record of pending approval requests

functions.description: Checks if RequestID and ClientNodeID combination already exists in pending requests that have not completed or errored
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through pending_requests. For each record, check if record.RequestID == RequestID AND record.ClientNodeID == ClientNodeID AND record.status IN [pending_event, approved]. Return true if match found, false otherwise
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Idle->Duplicate_RequestID_Detected transition to enforce SHOULD requirement about avoiding duplicate RequestIDs

functions.name: store_approval_request
functions.description: Stores approval request details in server's pending_requests table for later validation during CommissionNode
functions.algorithm_detail: Create new request_record with all parameters. Add to pending_requests table indexed by RequestID. Set record.status to pending_event, record.timestamp to current time
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Idle->Approval_Request_Accepted transition to persist request details

functions.name: generate_commissioning_request_result_event
functions.description: Generates fabric-sensitive CommissioningRequestResult event containing approval result
functions.algorithm_detail: Create event structure with fields: RequestID, ClientNodeID, StatusCode. Mark event as fabric-sensitive (visible only to nodes on same fabric). Send event to client via Matter event reporting mechanism
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Approval_Request_Accepted->Approval_Granted/Denied/Timeout transitions to notify client of approval result

functions.name: request_user_approval
functions.description: Optionally requests user approval for commissioning operation, displaying device identity information to user
functions.algorithm_detail: Display UI prompt to user showing Label, VendorID, ProductID. Wait for user response (approve/deny) or timeout. Return approval status based on user action
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Approval_Request_Accepted->Awaiting_User_Approval transition when server chooses to require user approval

functions.name: validate_commission_node_identity
functions.description: Verifies that CommissionNode command is sent from same NodeID and Fabric as RequestCommissioningApproval
functions.algorithm_detail: Compare identities: return (CommissionNode_NodeID == Approval_NodeID AND CommissionNode_FabricID == Approval_FabricID)
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Approval_Granted->CommissionNode_Identity_Mismatch transition to enforce identity binding security property

functions.name: validate_commission_node_requestid
functions.description: Verifies that RequestID exists in approved_requests table
functions.algorithm_detail: Search approved_requests for record with matching RequestID. Return true if found, false otherwise
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Approval_Granted->CommissionNode_RequestID_Invalid transition to validate RequestID

functions.name: check_approval_success
functions.description: Checks if CommissioningRequestResult event with StatusCode SUCCESS exists for given RequestID
functions.algorithm_detail: Search event_history for event where event.RequestID == RequestID AND event.StatusCode == SUCCESS. Return true if found, false otherwise
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Approval_Granted->CommissionNode_No_Approval_Success transition to enforce approval requirement

functions.name: check_already_responded
functions.description: Checks if ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow already sent for given RequestID
functions.algorithm_detail: Search response_history for entry where entry.RequestID == RequestID AND entry.response_sent == true. Return true if found, false otherwise
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Approval_Granted->CommissionNode_Already_Used transition to enforce single-use approval property

functions.name: generate_reverse_open_commissioning_window
functions.description: Creates ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow command message with PAKE parameters for client to open commissioning window
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct command message structure with command ID 0x02, populate fields: CommissioningTimeout (field 0), PAKEPasscodeVerifier (field 1), Discriminator (field 2), Iterations (field 3), Salt (field 4). Return encoded message
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Commissioning_Window_Preparation->Server_Window_Response_Sent transition to create response for client

functions.name: validate_pake_parameters
functions.description: Validates PAKE parameters are within secure ranges: Iterations in [1000, 100000], Salt length in [16, 32] bytes
functions.algorithm_detail: Check constraints: return (Iterations >= 1000 AND Iterations <= 100000 AND length(Salt) >= 16 AND length(Salt) <= 32)
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Commissioning_Window_Preparation->PAKE_Parameters_Invalid transition to validate cryptographic parameter security

functions.name: validate_discriminator
functions.description: Validates discriminator is within valid 12-bit range (max 4095)
functions.algorithm_detail: Check constraint: return (Discriminator <= 4095)
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Commissioning_Window_Preparation->PAKE_Parameters_Invalid transition to validate discriminator range

functions.name: start_response_timer
functions.description: Starts countdown timer for client to wait for ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow response
functions.algorithm_detail: Create timer object with initial value ResponseTimeoutSeconds. Start countdown at 1 second resolution. Return timer handle for later checking
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Approval_Granted->Client_Awaiting_Response transition to enforce timeout property

functions.name: match_device_identity
functions.description: Checks if device's VendorID and ProductID match expected values from approval request
functions.algorithm_detail: Retrieve device.VendorID and device.ProductID. Return (device.VendorID == VendorID AND device.ProductID == ProductID)
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Client_Window_Opening->Client_Device_Mismatch/Client_Commissioning_Window_Open transitions to verify correct device selected

functions.name: open_commissioning_window

parameters.name: device
parameters.description: Device on which to open commissioning window

functions.description: Opens commissioning window on target device using provided PAKE parameters. This is an alias to OpenCommissioningWindow command in Administrator Commissioning Cluster
functions.algorithm_detail: Invoke Administrator Commissioning Cluster's OpenCommissioningWindow command on device with provided parameters. Configure device to accept commissioning connections using PAKE authentication with specified parameters for CommissioningTimeout duration
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Client_Window_Opening->Client_Commissioning_Window_Open transition to establish commissioning access on device

functions.name: verify_device_attestation
functions.description: Performs Device Attestation Procedure to verify device's Device Attestation Certificate and extract verified VendorID and ProductID
functions.algorithm_detail: Execute Matter Device Attestation Procedure: retrieve Device Attestation Certificate (DAC), verify DAC signature chain against Product Attestation Authority, extract VendorID and ProductID from DAC, verify device signature. Return verification result containing VendorID and ProductID if successful
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Server_Commissioning_Device->Device_Attestation_Verification transition during concurrent commissioning flow

functions.name: check_basic_info_match

parameters.name: device_BasicInfo_VendorID
parameters.description: VendorID from device's Basic Information Cluster

parameters.name: device_BasicInfo_ProductID
parameters.description: ProductID from device's Basic Information Cluster

functions.description: Verifies that device's Basic Information Cluster VendorID and ProductID match values from approval request, as required during commissioning
functions.algorithm_detail: Compare identities: return (device_BasicInfo_VendorID == approval_VendorID AND device_BasicInfo_ProductID == approval_ProductID)
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Device_Attestation_Verification->Device_Identity_Mismatch/Commissioning_Complete transitions to enforce device identity verification security property

functions.name: abort_commissioning
functions.description: Aborts ongoing commissioning operation and cleans up commissioning state. Server SHALL NOT complete commissioning when this is called
functions.algorithm_detail: Terminate commissioning connection to device. Close any open commissioning windows. Remove device from pending commissioning list. Log abort reason. Clean up temporary commissioning state
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Device_Attestation_Verification->Device_Identity_Mismatch transition when VendorID/ProductID mismatch detected

functions.name: indicate_error_to_user
functions.description: Indicates error to user through UI or logging mechanism. Server SHOULD call this when commissioning fails due to VendorID/ProductID mismatch
functions.algorithm_detail: Display error_message through available user interface (screen, app notification, log). Implementation-specific but user must be able to see why commissioning failed
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Device_Identity_Mismatch->Commissioning_Aborted_With_Error transition to fulfill SHOULD requirement for error notification

functions.name: complete_commissioning

parameters.name: fabric
parameters.description: Fabric onto which device is commissioned

functions.description: Completes commissioning of device onto fabric after all verification checks passed
functions.algorithm_detail: Finalize device commissioning: install fabric credentials (NOC, ICAC, root CA) on device, assign NodeID, configure ACLs, complete operational certificate chain, add device to fabric's node list, close commissioning window
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Device_Attestation_Verification->Commissioning_Complete transition when all identity checks succeed

functions.name: get_device_attestation_certificate
functions.description: Retrieves Device Attestation Certificate from device during Device Attestation Procedure
functions.algorithm_detail: Request and receive DAC from device via Matter commissioning protocol. Verify certificate format and signature chain. Return parsed certificate containing VendorID and ProductID in subject fields
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Server_Commissioning_Device->Device_Attestation_Verification transition to obtain device identity credentials

functions.name: get_basic_info_vendor_id
functions.description: Reads VendorID attribute from device's Basic Information Cluster
functions.algorithm_detail: Send Read Attribute request to device for Basic Information Cluster (0x0028) attribute VendorID (0x0002). Parse and return uint16 VendorID value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Server_Commissioning_Device->Device_Attestation_Verification transition to retrieve device identity for verification

functions.name: get_basic_info_product_id
functions.description: Reads ProductID attribute from device's Basic Information Cluster
functions.algorithm_detail: Send Read Attribute request to device for Basic Information Cluster (0x0028) attribute ProductID (0x0003). Parse and return uint16 ProductID value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Server_Commissioning_Device->Device_Attestation_Verification transition to retrieve device identity for verification

functions.name: generate_failure_response
functions.description: Generates failure response to client command with specified status code
functions.algorithm_detail: Create Matter status response message with provided status_code. Send response to client that sent the command
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in various error state transitions to return failure status to client

functions.name: generate_success_response
functions.description: Generates SUCCESS response to RequestCommissioningApproval command
functions.algorithm_detail: Create Matter status response message with status_code = SUCCESS. Send response to client. Note: actual approval result comes later via CommissioningRequestResult event
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Idle->Approval_Request_Accepted transition per spec requirement that server always returns SUCCESS for correctly formatted request

functions.name: prepare_pake_parameters
functions.description: Prepares PAKE parameters for ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow command
functions.algorithm_detail: Generate or retrieve: CommissioningTimeout from configuration, PAKEPasscodeVerifier from secure storage or generation, Discriminator from device info, Iterations from security policy (1000-100000), Salt from secure random generator (16-32 bytes)
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Approval_Granted->Commissioning_Window_Preparation transition to prepare window parameters

functions.name: send_reverse_open_commissioning_window
functions.description: Sends ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow command from server to client
functions.algorithm_detail: Transmit window_message over CASE session to client. Mark request as responded in response_history
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Commissioning_Window_Preparation->Server_Window_Response_Sent transition to send window parameters to client

functions.name: initiate_concurrent_commissioning
functions.description: Initiates concurrent connection commissioning flow from server to device through opened commissioning window
functions.algorithm_detail: Establish commissioning connection to device using PAKE parameters from ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow. Begin Matter commissioning protocol flow: PAKE handshake, device attestation, operational credential provisioning
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Client_Commissioning_Window_Open->Server_Commissioning_Device transition to start commissioning device onto server's fabric

functions.name: extract_window_parameters
functions.description: Extracts PAKE parameters from ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow command for opening commissioning window
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow message. Extract fields: CommissioningTimeout (field 0), PAKEPasscodeVerifier (field 1), Discriminator (field 2), Iterations (field 3), Salt (field 4). Return as structured object
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Client_Awaiting_Response->Client_Window_Opening transition to parse received window parameters

functions.name: consider_responses_invalid
functions.description: Marks any future ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow responses as invalid due to timeout
functions.algorithm_detail: Set client state to reject any ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow messages for current RequestID. Clean up pending request state
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Client_Awaiting_Response->Client_Response_Timeout transition when ResponseTimeoutSeconds expires

functions.name: describe_parameter_error
functions.description: Generates descriptive error message for PAKE parameter validation failures
functions.algorithm_detail: Check each parameter: if Iterations < 1000 or > 100000, return 'Iterations out of range [1000, 100000]'. If length(Salt) < 16 or > 32, return 'Salt length out of range [16, 32]'. If Discriminator > 4095, return 'Discriminator exceeds 4095'. Return combined error message
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Commissioning_Window_Preparation->PAKE_Parameters_Invalid transition to provide error details

functions.name: abort_window_preparation
functions.description: Aborts ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow preparation due to invalid parameters
functions.algorithm_detail: Clean up window preparation state. Do not send ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow. Log error. Return failure to client
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Commissioning_Window_Preparation->PAKE_Parameters_Invalid transition when parameter validation fails

functions.name: generate_device_mismatch_error
functions.description: Generates error when client cannot find device matching approval VendorID/ProductID
functions.algorithm_detail: Log error indicating device with specified VendorID/ProductID not found or accessible. Abort commissioning window opening
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Client_Window_Opening->Client_Device_Mismatch transition when device selection fails

functions.name: generate_identity_mismatch_error_message
functions.description: Creates error message for VendorID/ProductID mismatch during commissioning
functions.algorithm_detail: Format error message: 'Device identity mismatch: approval requested VendorID=<approval.VendorID>, ProductID=<approval.ProductID>, but device reported VendorID=<device.VendorID>, ProductID=<device.ProductID>'. Return formatted string
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Device_Identity_Mismatch->Commissioning_Aborted_With_Error transition to provide detailed mismatch information

functions.name: clear_error_state
functions.description: Clears error state and returns FSM to Idle for new request
functions.algorithm_detail: Reset all error flags. Clear temporary state variables. Remove failed request from pending_requests table. Ready system for new RequestCommissioningApproval
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in various error state->Idle transitions to clean up after errors

functions.name: clear_approval_state
functions.description: Clears approval state after denial or timeout
functions.algorithm_detail: Remove request from pending_requests table. Clear approval_status. Reset event_generated flag. Ready system for new request
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Approval_Denied/Timeout->Idle transitions to clean up after approval failure

functions.name: clear_client_state
functions.description: Clears client-side state after error or timeout
functions.algorithm_detail: Stop any active timers. Clear window parameters. Reset response tracking flags. Ready client for new commissioning attempt
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in client error state->Idle transitions to clean up client state

functions.name: clear_commissioning_state
functions.description: Clears commissioning state after completion or abort
functions.algorithm_detail: Close commissioning windows. Clear device references. Remove from active commissioning table. Update request status to completed or failed. Ready for new commissioning request
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Commissioning_Aborted_With_Error/Complete->Idle transitions to clean up after commissioning ends

functions.name: reset_for_new_request
functions.description: Resets FSM state to accept new RequestCommissioningApproval
functions.algorithm_detail: Clear all state variables to initial values. Reset approval_status to none. Clear request_record. Set commissioning_window_status to closed
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Commissioning_Complete->Idle transition to prepare for next commissioning operation

functions.name: start_approval_timer
functions.description: Starts timer for user approval timeout
functions.algorithm_detail: Create and start timer with manufacturer-determined duration for user approval. When timer expires, trigger user_approval_timeout event
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Approval_Request_Accepted->Awaiting_User_Approval transition to enforce approval timeout

functions.name: generate_client_side_check_failure
functions.description: Handles client-side device category check failure (client should not have sent request)
functions.algorithm_detail: This represents client violating SHALL NOT requirement. Server may log the violation. In practice, if server receives such request, it should reject it
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Idle->Device_Category_Check_Failed transition when client violates device category precondition

definitions.definition: Certificate Authenticated Session Establishment - a secure authenticated session protocol in Matter using operational certificates to establish encrypted communication between nodes with verified identities
definitions.security_relevance: CRITICAL: All Commissioner Control Cluster commands require CASE session. Without CASE, commands fail with UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS or UNEXPECTED_ACCESS. Ensures only authenticated fabric members can request commissioning operations

definitions.definition: 64-bit unique identifier set by client in RequestCommissioningApproval to correlate approval request with subsequent CommissionNode command and server responses
definitions.security_relevance: HIGH: RequestID is security-critical for binding approval to commissioning operation. Duplicate RequestIDs create confusion. Stolen RequestIDs enable session hijacking. Must be unique per client

definitions.definition: Matter event that is only visible to nodes on the same fabric as the event source, enforcing fabric isolation boundaries
definitions.security_relevance: CRITICAL: CommissioningRequestResult event is fabric-sensitive, preventing cross-fabric information leakage about commissioning operations and device identities

definitions.definition: X.509 certificate containing device's VendorID and ProductID, signed by Product Attestation Authority, used to verify device authenticity during commissioning
definitions.security_relevance: CRITICAL: DAC verification ensures commissioned device is authentic and matches approval. Prevents counterfeit devices and device substitution attacks

definitions.definition: Matter cluster (0x0028) containing device metadata including VendorID and ProductID attributes that must match Device Attestation Certificate values
definitions.security_relevance: CRITICAL: Server checks Basic Information Cluster VendorID/ProductID against approval request during commissioning. Mismatch causes commissioning abort. Prevents device spoofing

definitions.definition: Password Authenticated Key Exchange - cryptographic protocol (SPAKE2+ in Matter) allowing two parties to establish shared secret using password-derived verifier without revealing password
definitions.security_relevance: CRITICAL: PAKE parameters (Iterations, Salt, PAKEPasscodeVerifier) must be in secure ranges. Weak parameters enable brute-force attacks on commissioning window

definitions.definition: Commissioning flow where server establishes connection to device through client-opened commissioning window to commission device onto server's fabric
definitions.security_relevance: HIGH: Enables reverse commissioning pattern for ecosystem-to-ecosystem Fabric Synchronization. Requires careful identity verification to prevent unauthorized commissioning

definitions.definition: Bitmap attribute indicating which device categories (e.g., FabricSynchronization) this Commissioner Control Cluster server supports for commissioning
definitions.security_relevance: MEDIUM: Controls what types of devices can be commissioned. Client must check this before sending approval request. Prevents unsupported device commissioning attempts

definitions.definition: Duration (30-120 seconds) client waits for ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow response before considering it invalid
definitions.security_relevance: HIGH: Enforces temporal bounds on commissioning flow. Responses after timeout are invalid, preventing replay attacks and time-shifted commissioning operations

definitions.definition: Server-to-client command (alias to OpenCommissioningWindow) providing PAKE parameters for client to open commissioning window on device, enabling concurrent commissioning
definitions.security_relevance: CRITICAL: Contains cryptographic material (PAKE parameters). Client must validate device identity before opening window. Server uses window to commission device

security_properties.description: All RequestCommissioningApproval and CommissionNode commands must be executed via authenticated CASE session, failing with UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS or UNEXPECTED_ACCESS otherwise
Client shall not request approval for devices whose category is not in SupportedDeviceCategories bitmap
Server should reject duplicate RequestID+ClientNodeID combinations while previous request is still pending (not completed or errored)
CommissionNode must be sent from same NodeID and FabricID as RequestCommissioningApproval to prevent session hijacking
CommissionNode cannot proceed without prior CommissioningRequestResult event with StatusCode SUCCESS for matching RequestID
Each approved RequestID can only be used once for ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow unless client obtains new approval
Client must receive ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow within ResponseTimeoutSeconds (30-120s) or consider response invalid
Server must verify device Basic Information Cluster VendorID and ProductID match approval request values, aborting commissioning on mismatch
CommissioningRequestResult events are fabric-sensitive, visible only to nodes on same fabric, preventing cross-fabric information leakage
ReverseOpenCommissioningWindow PAKE parameters must be within secure ranges: Iterations [1000, 100000], Salt [16, 32] bytes, Discriminator <= 4095
Client must open commissioning window on device matching VendorID and ProductID from approval request to prevent device substitution
Server shall not complete commissioning when device identity mismatch detected, and should indicate error to user

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: CASE protocol using ECDSA with operational certificates
cryptographic_operations.output: Authenticated encrypted session with session keys
assumptions: Operational certificates are valid and signed by trusted fabric CA
Session keys derived via secure key derivation
No certificate private keys are compromised

cryptographic_operations.output: Shared secret for commissioning session establishment
assumptions: SPAKE2+ implementation is secure against offline dictionary attacks
Iterations count provides sufficient computational hardness (>= 1000)
PAKEPasscodeVerifier is not leaked or guessable

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: ECDSA signature verification using X.509 certificate chain
cryptographic_operations.output: Verified device identity (VendorID, ProductID) from DAC
assumptions: Product Attestation Authority root certificates are trusted
Device has not been cloned (private key is unique)

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: Matter event encryption using fabric session keys
cryptographic_operations.output: Encrypted event visible only to fabric members
assumptions: Fabric session keys are not leaked across fabric boundaries
Event encryption provides confidentiality and integrity
Only fabric members have decryption keys
Fabric isolation is enforced at transport layer

security_assumptions.assumption: CASE session protocol is secure and provides mutual authentication between fabric members
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attacker could impersonate legitimate fabric member, send unauthorized RequestCommissioningApproval or CommissionNode commands, bypassing all access control checks

security_assumptions.assumption: Device Attestation Procedure correctly verifies device authenticity and VendorID/ProductID cannot be forged
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attacker could commission counterfeit devices with fake VendorID/ProductID, defeating device identity verification and supply chain security

security_assumptions.assumption: Fabric isolation is maintained - nodes on different fabrics cannot observe each other's encrypted traffic or events
security_assumptions.if_violated: Cross-fabric information leakage enables reconnaissance attacks, revealing commissioning operations and device identities to unauthorized fabrics

security_assumptions.assumption: Clock synchronization between client and server is sufficient for ResponseTimeoutSeconds enforcement
security_assumptions.if_violated: Severe clock skew causes timeout violations: client may reject valid responses or accept expired responses, enabling replay attacks

security_assumptions.assumption: PAKE implementation (SPAKE2+) is secure and Iterations/Salt parameters provide sufficient hardness against brute-force
security_assumptions.if_violated: Weak PAKE parameters enable offline dictionary attacks on commissioning window, allowing unauthorized access to commission devices

security_assumptions.assumption: Basic Information Cluster VendorID and ProductID attributes are immutable after device manufacture and match DAC
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attacker could modify Basic Information Cluster post-attestation, bypassing device identity checks and commissioning wrong device

security_assumptions.assumption: RequestID space is sufficiently large (uint64) to prevent accidental or brute-force collisions
security_assumptions.if_violated: RequestID collisions enable session hijacking where attacker guesses or observes victim's RequestID and steals approved commissioning slot

security_assumptions.assumption: No privileged node with Commissioner Control Cluster server access is compromised
security_assumptions.if_violated: Compromised administrator can bypass all protocol checks, approve arbitrary devices, and commission unauthorized nodes onto fabric

security_assumptions.assumption: Server enforces approval validity timeout (manufacturer-determined) to prevent indefinite approval reuse
security_assumptions.if_violated: Approved RequestIDs remain valid indefinitely, enabling time-shifted attacks where attacker delays CommissionNode to open window at unexpected time

security_assumptions.assumption: SupportedDeviceCategories bitmap accurately reflects server's commissioning capabilities
security_assumptions.if_violated: Client capability negotiation fails, causing either rejected requests for supported devices or attempted commissioning of unsupported devices
